critic_raw:
BASELINE ASSESSMENT:

Three confounds prevent attribution of the 6% watch-time increase to the new feed ranker.

First, a major creator promotion campaign with preferential push notification and banner placement launched in week 2 of the 4-week rollout. This independently increases session watch time through promoted high-quality sports-event content and notification-driven reactivation, regardless of which feed model is active.

Second, the report does not separate home-feed-originated sessions from notification-reactivated sessions. If notification-driven sessions (with promoted event content) have higher watch time than typical home-feed sessions, they inflate the aggregate metric in a way that has nothing to do with feed ranking quality.

Third, neither the old nor the new ranker was replayed on the same request logs. Traffic composition changed across the measurement window: more notification-driven sessions, more event-related queries. The comparison is between different traffic mixes, not the same traffic ranked differently.

The model may still be better for ordinary home-feed traffic. This claim requires entrypoint separation and log replay under controlled promotional state.

Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
